cards.front: What is the primary challenge in Non-Convex Optimization Convergence to Critical Points?
cards.back: Evaluating the convergence of non-convex optimization algorithms to critical points

cards.front: What is the difference between Local Convergence and Global Convergence in Non-Convex Optimization?
cards.back: Local Convergence: optimization algorithm converges to a local minimum, Global Convergence: optimization algorithm converges to the global minimum

cards.front: What is the role of Second-Order Methods in Non-Convex Optimization Convergence to Critical Points?
cards.back: Second-Order Methods: approximate Hessian matrix to improve convergence rate and accuracy

cards.front: How do Karush-Kuhn-Tucker (KKT) Conditions relate to Non-Convex Optimization Convergence to Critical Points?
cards.back: KKT Conditions: necessary conditions for a point to be a local minimum, maximum, or saddle point in non-convex optimization problems

cards.front: What is the significance of Line Search in Non-Convex Optimization Convergence to Critical Points?
cards.back: Line Search: determines the step size at each iteration to ensure convergence to a critical point
